speaker: Susan
content: How's Bongo doing?

speaker: Mike
content: I'm waiting to see if they'll have to operate.
mark: I’m waiting to see ... 我正等着看……
operate v. 操纵，操作；开刀，动手术；

speaker: Susan
content: Oh, god. I stopped and I got this for... I'm just so sorry for this.

speaker: Mike
content: I'm really sorry I snapped at you. I just...
mark: snap at sb 对人厉声说 snap v. 厉声说,怒气冲冲地说；断裂,绷断；突然失控

speaker: Susan
content: Oh, no, it's OK.

speaker: Vet
content: Good news, we won't have to do surgery.
mark: surgery n. 外科手术；do surgery ≈ operate

speaker: Susan
content: Oh, that's great.

speaker: Vet
content: We've given Bongo something to help pass the earring. When he does, would you like us to retrieve it for you?
mark: help do sth 帮助做某事 == help to do sth

speaker: Susan
content: I'm gonna say no.
mark: I'm gonna say no. 我要说不；我打算拒绝

speaker: Vet
content: OK, you should be able to take him home in about an hour.
mark: take sb home 带某人回家
in about an hour 大约一小时后

speaker: Susan
content: Wow, You must be so relieved.
mark: relieved adj. 放心的，宽慰的 relieve v. 缓解；减轻

speaker: Mike
content: I'm sorry, I was just really worried.

speaker: Susan
content: It was your dog, I get it.

speaker: Mike
content: Actually, he was my wife's dog, and one of the last things she said to me in the hospital before she died was to be sure I looked after him, and I promised her I would.
mark: one of the last things she said to me was to be sure I looked after him. 整个句子在描述过去的事，she said 和 looked要保持时态一致性。
be sure that... 确保……；务必……
look after sb 照顾、照料某人

speaker: Alice
content: And just like that, Susan could suddenly see something she'd never seen before. Mike Delfino was still in love with his late wife.
mark: be in love with sb 爱上某人；与某人相爱
late adj.已故的；晚期的，末期的；晚的，迟的；近日暮的，近深夜的

speaker: Mike
content: He meant so much to her. And if something had happened, I would've felt like I'd failed her. I know that sounds stupid, but...
mark: mean so much to sb 对某人来说意义重大；对某人非常重要
If something happened, I would feel that I failed her. 如果发生了什么事情，我会觉得我辜负了她
If something had happened, I would've felt like I had failed her. 要是当时发生了什么事情的话，我就会觉得我已经辜负了她
fail sb 让某人失望；有负于某人

speaker: Alice
content: And she knew right then neither she nor Eddie would be laying claim to his heart anytime soon.
mark: right then 就在那时；当时
neither nor 既不……也不……
would be doing 过去将来进行时，在过去的时间点看将来正在进行的动作
claim n/v. 声称，断言；索要，索赔
lay claim to... 宣称对…拥有权利；主张…是自己的 lay claim to his heart 宣称赢得他的芳心；声称占据他的感情
anytime soon 很快的任何时候；近期内：表示事情不会很快改善或发生。

speaker: Alice
content: So she decided, for now, she could settle for just being his friend.
mark: for now 目前；暂时
settle for sth 妥协接受某事物 settle v. 解决，结束；确定，决定；定居

speaker: Susan
content: No, it doesn't, not at all. This is for you, well, for Bongo, you give it to him.

speaker: Alice
content: Yes, as I look back at the world I left behind, it's all so clear to me, the beauty that waits to be unveiled, the mysteries that long to be uncovered.
mark: look back at sth 回顾，回忆（某事）；回头看，再看（某物） look back at the world 回望这个世界；回首看世界
unveil v. （首次）公开，揭示；为…揭幕，揭去…上的覆盖物；除去面纱，显露
mystery n. 难以理解的事物，奥秘；神秘的人
uncover v. 揭露，发现；

speaker: Alice
content: But people so rarely stop to take a look, they just keep moving. It's a shame, really, There's so much to see.
mark: rarely adv. 罕有，很少；异乎寻常地，极度
stop to take a look 停下来看一眼
It's a shame 这是一件憾事 shame n. 令人惋惜的事；羞耻，羞愧；
